instructions: Bhuna onion-tomato masala until dry; toss in paneer and peppers.

instructions: Pan-roast boiled eggs in a peppery onion-tomato masala until coated.

name: Ragi Roti
instructions: Knead ragi flour with warm water; pat into rotis and roast. Calcium-rich.

name: Oats Roti
instructions: Mix oats and wheat flour; knead and roast into soft rotis.

name: Coconut Rice
instructions: Toss cooked rice with a coconut, mustard and curry-leaf tempering.

name: Grilled Chicken Salad
instructions: Grill seasoned chicken; slice over crisp salad with a lemon-olive dressing.

name: Chicken Vegetable Soup
instructions: Simmer shredded chicken with vegetables and garlic into a clear soup.

name: Fish Coconut Curry
instructions: Simmer fish in a light coconut-milk curry with curry leaves.

name: Cheese and chilli sandwich
instructions: Spread butter on bread slices. Layer cheese, sliced green chilli, and vegetable slices. Toast in a sandwich maker or pan until golden and cheese melts. Cut and serve warm.

name: Egg sandwich (Ande ka sandwich)
instructions: Scramble or fry an egg. Spread butter on bread slices and layer with the cooked egg. Add salt and pepper to taste. Toast in a sandwich maker until golden. Cut diagonally and serve.

name: Cheese and pineapple sandwich (Cheese aur ananas ka sandwich)
instructions: Spread butter on bread slices. Layer grated cheese and pineapple pieces. Toast in a sandwich maker until cheese melts. Season with black pepper and serve warm.

name: Cheese and tomato sandwich (Cheese aur tamatar ke sandwich)
instructions: Spread butter on bread slices. Layer sliced tomato and grated cheese. Toast in a sandwich maker until cheese melts. Season with salt and pepper. Serve warm.

name: Chicken sandwich
instructions: Cook and shred chicken breast. Spread butter and mayonnaise on bread. Layer shredded chicken, lettuce, and tomato slices. Toast until golden. Cut and serve.

name: Peanut and tomato sandwich (Moongfali aur tamatar ka sandwich)
instructions: Spread butter on bread slices. Apply peanut butter to one slice and layer tomato slices on the other. Combine and toast in a sandwich maker. Cut and serve warm.

name: Rainbow sandwich
instructions: Spread butter on bread slices. Layer colorful vegetables—shredded carrot, cucumber slices, tomato, and cheese. Toast in a sandwich maker until golden. Cut and serve.

name: Club sandwich
instructions: Toast three slices of bread. Layer cheese, cucumber, tomato, and lettuce between slices. Spread butter between layers. Cut diagonally into triangles and secure with toothpicks. Serve.

name: Vegetarian club sandwich
instructions: Toast bread slices. Layer paneer, tomato, lettuce, and cucumber between slices with butter. Cut diagonally and secure with toothpicks. Serve fresh and warm.

name: Salami sandwich
instructions: Spread butter on bread slices. Layer salami slices, cheese, tomato, and lettuce. Toast in a sandwich maker until cheese melts. Cut and serve warm.

name: Vegetable and mayonnaise sandwich
instructions: Spread mayonnaise on bread slices. Layer sliced cucumber, tomato, and lettuce. Season with salt and pepper. Toast lightly or serve fresh. Cut and serve.

name: Egg and tomato sandwich (Ande aur tamatar ka sandwich)
instructions: Fry or scramble an egg. Spread butter on bread and layer with cooked egg and tomato slices. Add salt and pepper. Toast in a sandwich maker. Cut and serve.

name: Mushroom and cheese sandwich (toasted)
instructions: Sauté sliced mushrooms in butter until soft. Spread remaining butter on bread and layer with mushrooms and cheese. Toast in a sandwich maker. Season and serve warm.

name: Pea keema sandwich (toasted) (Matar aur keema ka sandwich)
instructions: Cook keema (minced meat) with spices. Mix in peas. Spread butter on bread and layer with keema mixture. Toast in a sandwich maker. Cut and serve hot.

name: Fried Egg
instructions: Heat butter in a pan. Crack egg into the pan and fry until whites are set and yolk is to desired doneness. Season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately.

name: Scrambled egg (Ande ki bhurji)
instructions: Beat eggs with salt and pepper. Heat butter in a pan and pour in the egg mixture. Stir continuously until eggs are cooked and fluffy. Add chopped green chilli if desired. Serve hot.

name: Baked egg
instructions: Beat eggs with milk, cheese, salt, and pepper. Pour into a buttered ramekin or baking dish. Bake at 180°C for 15-20 minutes until set and slightly puffed. Serve warm.

name: Plain omelette/omlet
instructions: Beat eggs with salt and pepper. Heat butter in a non-stick pan on medium heat. Pour in eggs and cook until mostly set. Fold in half and slide onto a plate. Serve immediately.

name: Stuffed egg omelette/omlet
instructions: Sauté mushrooms in butter. Beat eggs with salt and pepper. Make an omelette, add cooked mushrooms and cheese in the center before folding. Cook until cheese melts. Serve warm.

name: Brown stock
instructions: Sauté vegetables lightly. Simmer in water for 30-40 minutes. Strain to get clear broth. Season with salt and pepper. Serve hot as a light starter or side.

name: Chicken stock
instructions: Boil chicken pieces with vegetables and water for 45-60 minutes. Strain and discard solids. Season the resulting broth with salt and pepper. Serve hot.

name: Hot and sour soup
instructions: Heat broth and add diced mushrooms and tofu. Season with vinegar, soy sauce, and chilli. Thicken with cornstarch slurry. Adjust taste and serve piping hot.

name: Radish parantha/paratha (Mooli ka parantha/paratha)
instructions: Mix grated radish with flour, salt, chilli, and cumin. Knead dough and make a ball. Roll out into a thin circle, stuff with radish mixture, and fold. Cook on a hot griddle with butter until golden. Serve hot.

name: Cauliflower parantha/paratha (Phoolgobhi ka parantha/paratha)
instructions: Mix finely chopped cauliflower with flour, salt, chilli, and cumin. Knead dough, roll out, stuff with cauliflower mixture, and fold. Cook on a griddle with butter until golden. Serve hot.

name: Sprouted moong parantha/paratha
instructions: Mix sprouted moong with flour, salt, chilli, and cumin. Knead dough and make balls. Roll out, stuff with sprouted moong mixture, and fold. Cook on a griddle with butter. Serve hot.

name: Keema parantha/paratha
instructions: Cook keema with onion and spices until cooked. Mix with flour and salt to make dough. Roll out, stuff with keema filling, and fold. Cook on a griddle with butter until golden. Serve hot.

name: Paneer parantha/paratha
instructions: Crumble paneer and mix with salt, chilli, and cumin. Knead dough with flour. Roll out, stuff with paneer mixture, and fold. Cook on a griddle with butter until golden. Serve hot.

name: Besan and spinach parantha/paratha (Besan aur palak ka parantha/paratha)
instructions: Mix besan, spinach, and spices into a dough with whole wheat flour and ghee. Roll out thin and cook on a griddle until golden and crispy. Serve hot with yogurt or pickle.

name: Boiled rice (Uble chawal)
instructions: Rinse rice and boil in salted water until soft and fluffy. Drain excess water. Serve as an accompaniment to curries.

name: Plain pulao
instructions: Heat ghee and temper with whole spices. Add rice and toast briefly. Pour water and cook covered until tender. Fluff with a fork.

name: Mixed vegetable pulao
instructions: Heat ghee and temper with cumin. Add mixed vegetables and cook for 2 minutes. Add rice and toast lightly. Pour water and cook covered until done.

name: Mushroom pulao
instructions: Heat ghee and sauté sliced mushrooms until golden. Add rice and toast. Pour water and cook covered until rice is tender. Stir gently.

name: Sprouted moong pulao
instructions: Heat ghee and temper with cumin and green chili. Add sprouted moong and stir-fry briefly. Add rice and toast. Pour water and cook covered.

name: Navratan pulao
instructions: Heat ghee and fry cashews and raisins until golden. Add mixed vegetables and cook briefly. Add rice and toast. Pour water and cook covered until tender.

name: Mutton biryani/biriyani
instructions: Marinate mutton in yogurt and spices. Layer with partially cooked rice. Cover and cook on high heat until steam forms, then low heat until done. Let rest 5 minutes.

name: Vegetable biryani/biriyani
instructions: Cook vegetables with spices. Layer with partially cooked basmati rice. Cover and steam until rice is tender. Garnish with mint before serving.

name: Tamarind rice (Chintapandu pulihora/Puliyodharai/Puli sadam/Huli anna)
instructions: Cook rice until soft. Heat oil and temper mustard seeds and red chili. Mix in tamarind paste and cooked rice. Toss well and serve warm.

name: Spanish rice
instructions: Cook rice. Heat oil and stir-fry vegetables. Mix in cooked rice and season with salt and pepper. Toss until heated through.

name: Chinese fried rice
instructions: Heat oil in a wok. Scramble eggs and remove. Stir-fry mixed vegetables. Add cooked rice and soy sauce. Toss well and serve hot.

name: Macroni cheese pie
instructions: Cook pasta and mix with cheese sauce made from butter, flour, and milk. Transfer to a baking dish, top with breadcrumbs. Bake at 180°C until golden.

name: Vegetable chowmein
instructions: Cook noodles until al dente. Heat oil, stir-fry garlic and ginger, then vegetables. Add noodles and soy sauce. Toss until well coated.

name: Chicken chowmein
instructions: Cook noodles. Heat oil and stir-fry garlic, ginger, and diced chicken until cooked. Add vegetables and noodles. Toss with soy sauce.

name: Cheese noodle ring
instructions: Cook noodles and mix with cheese sauce. Mold in a ring shape in a buttered pan. Top with breadcrumbs and bake until golden at 180°C.

name: Penne platter
instructions: Cook penne until al dente. Sauté garlic and vegetables in olive oil. Add tomato sauce. Toss with penne and finish with basil and cheese.

name: Fettuccine with spinach sauce
instructions: Cook fettuccine. Blend cooked spinach with cream and garlic. Heat sauce gently with butter. Toss with pasta and top with parmesan.

name: Naan
instructions: Mix flour, yogurt, salt, sugar, and baking powder. Knead into soft dough and let rest 2 hours. Roll and cook on a hot skillet until puffed and spotted.

name: Semolina dosa (Suji/Rava dosa)
instructions: Mix semolina with yogurt and let rest 15 minutes. Add green chili and cumin. Spread on hot griddle and cook until golden on both sides.

name: Paneer kaathi roll
instructions: Grill or pan-fry marinated paneer cubes. Layer in roti with grilled vegetables and mint chutney. Roll tightly and serve warm.

name: Washed moong dal (Dhuli moong ki dal)
instructions: Soak dal briefly and cook with turmeric until soft. Temper with hot oil infused with cumin seeds and green chili. Stir into dal.

name: Washed urad dal (Dhuli urad ki dal)
instructions: Cook soaked urad dal with turmeric until soft and creamy. Temper with oil, mustard seeds, and green chili. Mix well and serve hot.

name: Split bengal gram with bottle gourd (Channa dal with ghiya/lauki)
instructions: Cook soaked channa dal until half-done. Add cubed bottle gourd and turmeric. Continue cooking until tender. Temper with oil and cumin.

name: Dry washed urad
instructions: Dry roast urad dal until fragrant. Temper hot oil with cumin and red chili. Pour over dal and add salt. Mix gently.

name: Mixed dal
instructions: Soak and cook mixed dals together with turmeric until soft. Temper with hot oil infused with cumin seeds. Stir and serve.

name: Whole moong (Moong ki dal)
instructions: Soak whole moong and cook with turmeric until tender but not mushy. Temper with oil, cumin, and green chili. Mix gently.

name: Whole masoor (Masoor ki dal)
instructions: Cook soaked whole masoor with turmeric until soft. Temper with hot oil and mustard seeds. Stir well and serve hot.

name: Whole moth (Moth ki dal)
instructions: Soak and cook whole moth with turmeric until tender. Heat oil with cumin seeds and pour over cooked moth. Mix and serve.

name: Whole urad (Urad ki dal)
instructions: Cook soaked whole urad with turmeric until soft. Temper with hot oil, cumin, and green chili. Stir and serve warm.

name: Moti mahal dal (Urad rajmah mix dal)
instructions: Soak both dals and cook together with turmeric until very soft and creamy. Temper with hot ghee and cumin seeds. Mix well.

name: Black channa curry/Bengal gram curry (Kale chane ki curry)
instructions: Cook black chickpeas until soft. Sauté onions, add ginger-garlic paste, tomatoes, and spices. Add cooked chickpeas and simmer until thick.

name: Chickpeas curry (Safed channa curry)
instructions: Cook chickpeas until tender. Sauté onions, add ginger-garlic paste and tomatoes. Add cooked chickpeas with spices and simmer until thick.

name: Lobia curry
instructions: Cook black-eyed peas until tender. Sauté onions, add ginger-garlic paste and tomatoes. Add cooked peas with spices and simmer.

name: Soyabean curry
instructions: Cook soybeans until tender. Sauté onions, add ginger-garlic paste and tomatoes. Add cooked soybeans with spices and simmer.

name: Kidney bean curry (Rajmah curry)
instructions: Cook kidney beans until very soft. Sauté onions, add ginger-garlic paste and tomatoes. Add beans with spices and simmer until thick.

name: Sambar
instructions: Cook pigeon peas until soft. Add sambar powder and vegetables. Add tamarind paste. Temper with oil, mustard seeds, and curry leaves.

name: Khatta channa
instructions: Cook chickpeas until tender. Mix with tamarind paste, amchur powder, and ginger. Heat oil with cumin and pour over. Serve cold or warm.

name: Sprouted moong dal chat
instructions: Mix sprouted moong with finely chopped tomato, onion, and green chili. Add lime juice and salt. Toss gently and serve immediately.

name: Potato cauliflower (Aloo gobhi)
instructions: Cut vegetables into small pieces. Sauté onions and ginger. Add potatoes and cauliflower with turmeric. Cook until tender and serve.

name: Cabbage and peas (Pattagobhi aur matar)
instructions: Heat oil and add cumin seeds. Add diced onion and cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomato pieces. Add shredded cabbage and peas, then season with turmeric and salt. Cook on medium heat until vegetables are tender, about 10-12 minutes.

name: Carrot and fenugreek leaves (Gajar methi)
instructions: Heat oil and temper with cumin. Sauté onion until light brown. Add ginger-garlic paste, then diced carrot and fresh fenugreek leaves. Cook with tomato, turmeric, and salt for 12-15 minutes until carrot is cooked through.

name: Brinjal bhartha (Baingan ka bhartha)
instructions: Roast eggplant over flame or in oven until charred and soft. Peel off the skin and mash the flesh. Heat oil and sauté onion until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste, tomato, and the mashed eggplant. Cook on high heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring frequently to incorporate flavors.

name: Beans with coconut (Nariyal aur sem/phali; Beans thoran)
instructions: Cut beans into thin pieces. Heat oil and add cumin seeds. Add beans and dry roast for 2-3 minutes. Add fresh grated coconut, turmeric, and salt. Stir well and cook covered for 6-8 minutes on medium heat until beans are tender.

name: Cauliflower with coconut (Nariyal ke saath phoolgobhi)
instructions: Chop cauliflower into florets. Heat oil and add cumin seeds. Add cauliflower florets and dry roast for 3 minutes. Add grated coconut, turmeric, and salt. Cook covered on medium heat for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally.

name: Carrot and cabbage with coconut (Nariyal ke saath pattagobhi aur gajar)
instructions: Julienne carrot and shred cabbage. Heat oil and add cumin seeds. Add carrot and cabbage, dry roast for 2 minutes. Stir in grated coconut, turmeric, and salt. Cover and cook for 6-8 minutes on medium heat until vegetables are just tender.

name: Stuffed tomatoes (Bharwa tamatar)
instructions: Hollow out tomatoes carefully and chop the insides. Sauté onion, add ginger-chili paste and chopped tomato pulp, then mix in crumbled paneer and capsicum. Stuff the mixture back into tomatoes. Bake at 200°C for 15-18 minutes or cook in a covered pan.

name: Pea paneer curry (Matar paneer)
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onion until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomato. Cook until oil separates. Add peas and paneer cubes. Season with garam masala and salt. Simmer for 8-10 minutes until paneer is heated through and flavors are well combined.

name: Pea mushroom curry (Matar mushroom)
instructions: Heat oil and sauté cumin. Add diced onion and cook until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomato pieces. Add sliced mushroom and peas. Season with turmeric, chili powder, and salt. Cook for 10-12 minutes on medium heat.

name: Pea curry (Matar ki sabzi)
instructions: Heat oil and add cumin seeds. Sauté onion until light brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add tomato and cook until soft. Add peas and season with turmeric, garam masala, and salt. Simmer for 8-10 minutes.

name: Pea vadi curry
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onion until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomato. Add pea vadi pieces (cut diagonally) and fresh peas. Season with turmeric and garam masala. Simmer covered for 10-12 minutes until vadi is cooked through.

name: Paneer curry
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onion until light brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomato. Cook until oil separates. Add paneer cubes and season with garam masala, kasuri methi, and salt. Simmer for 5-7 minutes, stirring gently.

name: Lotus stem curry (Kamal kakdi curry)
instructions: Peel and slice lotus stem, then soak in water to prevent browning. Heat oil and sauté onion until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomato. Add drained lotus stem and season with turmeric and chili powder. Cook covered for 12-15 minutes until tender.

name: Baked vegetables
instructions: Cut vegetables into bite-sized pieces. Toss with olive oil, minced garlic, salt, pepper, and dried herbs. Spread on a baking sheet and bake at 220°C for 20-25 minutes, stirring halfway through.

name: Cauliflower musallam (Phoolgobhi musallam)
instructions: Blanch cauliflower florets for 3 minutes. Heat oil and add cumin. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add yogurt and spices. Add cauliflower florets and simmer covered for 8-10 minutes on low heat.

name: Baked vegetables with spinach
instructions: Sauté spinach with minced garlic until wilted. Mix with blanched broccoli, diced zucchini, and carrot. Top with grated cheese and bake at 200°C for 20 minutes until golden and cooked through.

name: Baked brinjal in tomato sauce
instructions: Slice eggplant and arrange in a baking dish. Make tomato sauce by cooking onion, garlic, tomato, and herbs. Pour sauce over eggplant. Bake at 200°C for 25-30 minutes until eggplant is soft and sauce is bubbly.

name: Spinach paneer (Palak paneer)
instructions: Blanch spinach and blend to a paste. Heat oil and add cumin. Sauté onion, add ginger-garlic paste and tomato. Cook until oil separates. Add spinach paste and paneer cubes. Season with garam masala and simmer for 5-7 minutes.

name: Sarson ka saag
instructions: Blanch mustard greens and spinach, then puree. Heat oil and add cumin. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chili. Cook the puree for 8-10 minutes on medium heat, stirring frequently. Season with salt and finish with a drizzle of ghee if desired.

name: Avial
instructions: Cut vegetables into thin sticks. Heat oil and add cumin seeds. Add ginger-chili paste and vegetables. Cook on medium heat for 4-5 minutes. Add fresh coconut paste and cook until vegetables are just tender, about 3-4 minutes. Finish with coconut oil.

name: Al yakhani
instructions: Peel and cube bottle gourd. Heat oil and add cumin. Sauté ginger-garlic paste for 1 minute. Add bottle gourd and paneer pieces. Season with garam masala and salt. Cook covered for 10-12 minutes on medium heat until gourd is tender.

name: Shahi paneer
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onion until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add tomato and cook until soft. Blend until smooth. Return to heat, add paneer cubes and cream. Season with garam masala and simmer for 5-7 minutes.

name: Methi malai paneer
instructions: Blanch fenugreek leaves and puree. Heat oil and sauté onion until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook briefly. Add fenugreek puree and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add paneer cubes, cream, and garam masala. Simmer for 5-7 minutes.

name: Kadhai Paneer
instructions: Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pan (kadhai). Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add cubed paneer and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add capsicum chunks and onion. Add tomato and garam masala. Cook on high heat for 4-5 minutes, stirring frequently.

name: Roghan josh
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onion until dark golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 2 minutes. Add yogurt and tomato, cook until oil separates. Add paneer cubes and garam masala. Simmer covered for 8-10 minutes on low heat.

name: Spinach mutton (Palak mutton)
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onion until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add mutton pieces and cook until browned. Add tomato and cook until oil separates. Add blanched spinach puree and garam masala. Simmer for 15-20 minutes until mutton is tender.

name: Pea keema curry (Matar keema ki sabzi)
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onion until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add ground mutton and cook until browned and crumbly. Add tomato and peas. Season with turmeric and garam masala. Simmer for 10-12 minutes until peas are cooked.

name: Kashmiri mutton koftas (Gushtaba)
instructions: Grind mutton with onion, ginger, garlic, and garam masala. Form into balls (koftas) and deep fry until golden. Make a yogurt-based gravy by cooking onion, ginger-garlic paste, yogurt, and spices. Add fried koftas and simmer for 5-7 minutes.

name: Mutton yakhni
instructions: Heat oil and add whole spices (bay leaf, cardamom). Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add mutton pieces and sear on all sides. Add water and turmeric. Pressure cook for 20-25 minutes on high heat until mutton is very tender.

name: Chicken yakhni
instructions: Heat oil and add whole spices. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add chicken pieces and sear. Add water, turmeric, and salt. Pressure cook for 10-12 minutes on high until chicken is fully cooked and flavors are infused.

name: Chicken curry
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onion until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add chicken pieces and cook until browned. Add tomato and cook until soft. Season with turmeric and garam masala. Simmer covered for 12-15 minutes until chicken is cooked.

name: Fish curry (Machli curry)
instructions: Heat oil and sauté onion until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add tomato and cook until soft. Season with turmeric and chili powder. Gently add fish pieces and simmer covered for 8-10 minutes until fish is cooked through.

name: Baked fish with cheese sauce
instructions: Make a cheese sauce by melting butter, adding flour, then milk and grated cheese. Season with salt and pepper. Place fish fillets in a baking dish, pour cheese sauce over them, and bake at 200°C for 15-20 minutes until fish is cooked and sauce is golden.

name: Russian salad
instructions: Boil and dice potato and carrot. Mix with cooked peas and diced apple. Bind with mayonnaise and lemon juice. Season with salt and pepper. Chill before serving.

name: Deviled egg
instructions: Boil eggs and halve them. Remove yolks and mix with mayonnaise, mustard, paprika, lemon juice, salt, and pepper to make the filling. Pipe or spoon the mixture into egg white halves. Garnish with paprika and serve chilled.

name: Mixed pulse and vegetable salad
instructions: Soak and sprout mixed pulses (moong, chana, urad). Chop vegetables finely. Toss sprouts and vegetables together. Dress with lemon juice, salt, and fresh coriander.

name: Tossed salad
instructions: Wash and chop fresh greens and vegetables. Toss together gently. Dress with olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper just before serving.

name: Mixed vegetable salad with curd sauce
instructions: Chop vegetables finely. Whisk curd with lemon juice, salt, and cumin powder. Mix vegetables with the curd sauce and chill before serving.

name: Tomato onion raita (Tamatar aur pyaaz ka raita)
instructions: Chop tomato and onion finely. Whisk curd until smooth. Mix curd with tomato, onion, cumin powder, salt, and red chili powder. Serve chilled.

name: Peanut raita (Mungfali ka raita)
instructions: Roast peanuts lightly and crush coarsely. Whisk curd until smooth. Mix curd with crushed peanuts, cumin powder, salt, and red chili powder. Serve chilled.

name: Sprouted moong raita
instructions: Sprout moong dal for 2-3 days. Whisk curd until smooth. Mix curd with sprouted moong, cumin powder, salt, red chili powder, and lemon juice. Serve chilled.

name: Bottle gourd raita (Ghiya/Lauki ka raita)
instructions: Grate bottle gourd and squeeze out excess water. Whisk curd smooth. Mix curd with grated gourd, cumin powder, salt, and red chili powder. Serve chilled.

name: Carrot and spinach raita (Gajar aur palak ka raita)
instructions: Finely grate carrot and blanch spinach briefly, then chop. Whisk curd smooth. Mix curd with carrot, spinach, cumin powder, salt, red chili powder, and lemon juice. Serve chilled.

name: Mint raita (Pudinay ka raita)
instructions: Chop fresh mint leaves finely. Whisk curd until smooth. Mix curd with mint, cumin powder, salt, red chili powder, and lemon juice. Serve chilled.

name: Paneer shaslik/tikka
instructions: Cut paneer into chunks. Marinate with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, cumin, turmeric, and salt for 30 minutes. Thread paneer and vegetables onto skewers. Grill or bake at 200°C until golden.

name: Minced meat patties
instructions: Mix ground meat with breadcrumbs, egg, minced onion, salt, and pepper. Form into patties. Pan-fry in oil over medium-high heat until golden brown on both sides.

name: Cheese patties
instructions: Mix grated cheese with breadcrumbs, egg, minced onion, salt, and pepper. Form into patties. Pan-fry in oil over medium-high heat until golden brown on both sides.

name: Hot cheese souffle
instructions: Melt butter, add flour to make a roux, whisk in milk. Stir in grated cheese until smooth. Remove from heat, whisk in egg yolks. Fold in beaten egg whites. Bake at 190°C for 25-30 minutes until puffed and golden.

name: Hot fish souffle
instructions: Steam and flake cooked fish. Make a béchamel with butter, flour, and milk. Mix flaked fish into béchamel. Fold in beaten egg whites. Bake at 190°C for 25-30 minutes until golden and puffed.

name: Hot spinach souffle
instructions: Blanch and chop spinach finely. Make a béchamel with butter, flour, and milk. Mix spinach and grated cheese into béchamel. Fold in beaten egg whites. Bake at 190°C for 25-30 minutes until puffed and golden.

name: Dhokla
instructions: Mix gram flour, yogurt, ginger, and green chilli into a batter. Season with salt and ferment for 30 minutes. Pour into oiled steamer and steam for 8-10 minutes until cooked through. Temper with mustard seeds and curry leaves, then cut into pieces.

name: Moong dal stuffed cheela/chilla (Moong dal ka cheela/chilla)
instructions: Soak and grind moong dal with gram flour, salt, and green chilli. Prepare vegetable stuffing with cooked vegetables. Make thin crepes and fill with stuffing. Cook on a griddle until golden on both sides.

name: Paneer stuffed cheela/chilla
instructions: Prepare gram flour batter with salt and green chilli. Crumble and cook paneer with cumin and coriander as stuffing. Make thin crepes and fill with paneer mixture. Cook on griddle until light brown.

name: Indian style egg bhujia (Anda bhujia (Indian style))
instructions: Heat oil and sauté diced onion and tomato. Add beaten eggs, turmeric, salt, and green chilli. Scramble continuously until fully cooked. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: French omelette/omlet
instructions: Beat eggs with milk, salt, and pepper until fluffy. Heat butter in a non-stick pan. Pour eggs and cook gently while stirring from the edges until creamy. Fold in half and serve immediately.

name: Puffy omelette/omlet
instructions: Separate egg whites from yolks. Beat whites until stiff peaks form. Fold yolks, salt, and pepper into whites. Pour into buttered pan and cook over low heat until set and puffy.

name: Orange omelette/omlet
instructions: Grate or dice carrot finely. Beat eggs with milk, salt, and pepper. Add carrot to beaten eggs. Heat butter and cook the mixture on a griddle or pan until set and light golden.

name: Cheese and mushroom omelette/omlet
instructions: Sauté diced mushrooms in butter until tender. Beat eggs with salt, pepper, and milk. Pour into buttered pan, add mushrooms and cheese. Fold when partially cooked and serve hot.

name: Spanish omelette/omlet
instructions: Cook diced potato and onion in butter. Add diced bell pepper and cook until soft. Beat eggs with salt, pepper, and paprika. Pour over vegetables and cook until set on both sides.

name: Chicken stew
instructions: Cook diced chicken in oil with ginger-garlic paste and onions. Add tomato paste and spices. Add vegetables and coconut milk. Simmer until chicken is tender and vegetables are cooked through.

name: Veg paneer stew
instructions: Cube paneer and vegetables. Sauté onion and ginger, add tomato and spices. Add vegetables and cook until soft. Add paneer cubes and simmer gently for 5-7 minutes without breaking them.

name: Instant idli (with semolina)
instructions: Mix semolina and yogurt. Add salt and baking powder just before steaming. Pour into oiled moulds. Steam for 5-7 minutes until set. Serve warm with chutney.

name: Plain dosa
instructions: Soak rice and urad dal separately, then grind together with fenugreek to a thin batter. Ferment overnight. Heat a griddle, pour thin layer of batter, spread evenly. Cook until crispy and light brown on both sides.

name: Masala dosa mixed vegetable fillings
instructions: Prepare dosa batter and ferment. Cook potato, onion, and carrot with mustard, curry leaves, and turmeric. Spread dosa on griddle and fill with vegetable mixture. Fold and serve with sambar and chutney.

name: Masala dosa paneer fillings
instructions: Prepare fermented dosa batter. Sauté crumbled paneer with onion and green chilli. Spread dosa on griddle, fill with paneer mixture, fold, and serve hot with sambar.

name: Uttapam
instructions: Prepare dosa batter and let it rest. Spread thick layer on griddle. Top with onion, tomato, green chilli, and curry leaves. Cook until bottom is crispy. Serve with sambar and chutney.

name: Sour lentils (Khatti dal)
instructions: Cook lentils until soft. Temper cumin and asafoetida in oil, add ginger and green chilli. Mix with cooked lentils and tomato. Simmer for 5 minutes and adjust seasoning.

name: Urad special dehusked
instructions: Soak and cook dehusked urad dal until tender. Temper cumin and asafoetida in oil, add ginger, onion, and green chilli. Mix into cooked dal and simmer gently.

name: Arhar with spinach (Arhar dal aur palak)
instructions: Cook arhar dal until soft. Blanch and chop spinach. Temper cumin in oil, add ginger-garlic, tomato, and turmeric. Mix in cooked dal and spinach. Simmer for 5 minutes.

name: Rasam with tamarind (Puli rasam/ Chintapandu rasam/ Charu/Saaru)
instructions: Soak tamarind in hot water and extract pulp. Heat oil, add cumin seeds and curry leaves, then add tamarind pulp and tomatoes. Simmer for 10-15 minutes. Season with coriander, black pepper, and salt to taste.

name: Rasam with lemon (Nimmakaya rasam/Nimmakaya charu/Elumichai rasam/Nimbe hannina saaru)
instructions: Heat oil and add cumin seeds, curry leaves, and crushed black pepper. Pour hot water, add tomato paste, and bring to boil. Add fresh lemon juice and simmer for 5 minutes. Season with salt and serve.

name: Green gram whole with baghar (Sabut moong dal with tadka)
instructions: Soak and boil moong dal until tender. Heat oil and add cumin seeds, dried red chili, and asafoetida. Add boiled dal with tomatoes and ginger, simmer for 10 minutes. Season with salt and lemon juice.

name: Urad sabut special
instructions: Soak urad dal, toast lightly and cook until soft. In a pan, temper oil with cumin, add onions, garlic, and ginger. Add cooked dal with tomatoes and cream, simmer until thick. Season and serve.

name: Kabuli channa curry
instructions: Boil chickpeas until tender. Heat oil and add cumin, onions, ginger, and garlic. Add tomatoes and cooked chickpeas, simmer for 15 minutes. Add coriander powder and garam masala, season with salt.

name: Besan gatte curry
instructions: Mix gram flour with spices and yogurt to make dough, roll into logs and boil. Heat oil, temper with cumin, add onions, ginger, and garlic. Add tomato sauce, then cooked gatte, simmer for 10 minutes.

name: Dry masala chops
instructions: Mash cottage cheese and mix with ginger, green chili, breadcrumbs, and spices. Form into chops, shallow-fry in oil until golden and crispy on both sides.

name: Mutton korma
instructions: Marinate mutton in yogurt with ginger-garlic paste. Heat oil, cook onions until golden, add meat and yogurt mixture. Simmer until tender, add crushed almonds and cardamom. Cook on low heat for 1-1.5 hours.

name: Minced meat ball curry
instructions: Mix minced meat with onion, ginger, garlic, breadcrumbs, and spices. Form into balls. Heat oil and cook meatballs until browned, add tomato sauce and simmer for 20 minutes until cooked through.

name: Soya chunks korma (Nutrinugget korma)
instructions: Soak soya chunks in hot water. Heat oil, cook onions, add ginger and garlic. Add soya chunks, tomato sauce, and yogurt. Simmer until tender, finish with cream and garam masala.

name: Mushroom matar
instructions: Heat oil, add cumin and sliced onions, cook until golden. Add ginger, then mushrooms and peas. Add tomatoes and spices, simmer for 15 minutes until vegetables are tender.

name: Soya chunks and peas (Nutrinugget matar)
instructions: Soak soya chunks. Heat oil, add cumin and onions. Add soaked soya chunks, peas, ginger, and tomatoes. Cook for 15-20 minutes until tender, season with salt and spices.

name: Spinach mushroom (Palak mushroom)
instructions: Blanch spinach, cool, and puree. Heat oil, add ginger and mushrooms, cook until soft. Add spinach puree and cream, simmer for 10 minutes. Season with salt and green chili.

name: Roast chicken
instructions: Marinate chicken pieces in oil, lemon juice, ginger-garlic paste, and spices for at least 30 minutes. Roast in oven at 200°C for 40-45 minutes until golden and cooked through, turning halfway.

name: Shahi chicken masala
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt with spices. Heat oil, cook onions until golden, add ginger-garlic paste. Add marinated chicken and tomato sauce, simmer for 30 minutes. Finish with cream and garam masala.

name: Tomato chicken
instructions: Heat oil, add cumin and sliced onions. Add ginger and diced chicken, cook until browned. Add crushed tomatoes and spices, simmer for 20 minutes until chicken is cooked and sauce thickens.

name: Creamy chicken
instructions: Heat oil and cook chicken pieces until light brown. Add ginger and green chili, then yogurt and cream. Simmer for 20 minutes until chicken is tender and sauce thickens, season with salt.

name: Broccoli chicken ala king
instructions: Cook chicken and cut into pieces. Blanch broccoli. Heat butter, sauté onion and garlic, add mushrooms and chicken. Add cream and broccoli, simmer for 10 minutes. Season with salt and pepper.

name: Ginger chicken
instructions: Marinate chicken with cornstarch and soy sauce. Heat oil, add ginger and garlic, stir-fry chicken until cooked. Add vinegar, more soy sauce, and green chili. Simmer until sauce clings to chicken.

name: Chicken korma
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt with ginger-garlic paste. Heat oil, cook onions until golden, add chicken and yogurt mix. Simmer for 30 minutes, add crushed almonds and cardamom, finish with cream.

name: Bengal fish curry (Bengali machli curry)
instructions: Heat oil, add onions and ginger, cook until soft. Add fish pieces and turmeric, cook for 2 minutes. Add tomatoes and red chili, simmer for 10 minutes. Add coconut milk and cook until fish is tender.

name: Prawn curry (with coconut) (Jhinga curry)
instructions: Heat oil, add curry leaves and onions, cook until golden. Add prawns with turmeric and red chili, cook for 3 minutes. Add coconut milk and simmer for 10 minutes until prawns are cooked through.

name: Fish pie
instructions: Cook fish and flake it. Boil potatoes and make mash with cream and butter. Layer fish with white sauce and potato mash in a baking dish, top with breadcrumbs. Bake at 180°C for 20 minutes.

name: Crispy baked fish
instructions: Pat fish dry and season with salt and pepper. Dip in beaten egg, coat with breadcrumbs mixed with garlic powder. Place on oiled baking tray and bake at 200°C for 20 minutes until golden and crispy.

name: Fish souffle
instructions: Cook and flake fish, mix with egg yolks, butter, cream, and breadcrumbs. Fold in stiffly beaten egg whites. Bake in a buttered dish at 180°C for 25-30 minutes until puffed and golden.

name: Potato curry (Aloo ki sabzi)
instructions: Heat oil, add cumin and onions. Add ginger, then diced potatoes and turmeric. Cook for 10 minutes, add tomatoes and coriander. Simmer for 15 minutes until potatoes are tender.

name: Vegetable curry with coconut
instructions: Heat oil, add cumin and onions. Add ginger and chopped vegetables with turmeric, cook for 5 minutes. Add coconut milk and green chili, simmer for 15 minutes until vegetables are cooked.

name: Curd with potatoes (Dahi aloo)
instructions: Boil diced potatoes. Heat oil, add cumin and onions, then ginger and green chili. Add potatoes, yogurt, and coriander. Stir gently and simmer for 10 minutes, season with salt.

name: Spinach and potato (Palak aloo)
instructions: Boil potatoes. Heat oil, add cumin, ginger, and green chili. Add chopped spinach and boiled potatoes, cook for 10 minutes. Add crushed tomatoes and simmer for 5 more minutes.

name: Mooli bhujia
instructions: Grate radish and squeeze out excess moisture. Heat oil, add cumin and asafoetida, add grated radish with turmeric and red chili. Cook on high heat for 10 minutes, stirring frequently, finish with lemon juice.

name: Bathua bhujia
instructions: Chop bathua greens finely. Heat oil, add cumin and asafoetida, add chopped greens with ginger and red chili. Cook on high heat for 8 minutes, stirring constantly. Season with salt and lemon juice.

name: Cabbage rolls (dry) ((Pattagobhi rolls) (dry))
instructions: Blanch cabbage leaves. Mix cottage cheese with ginger, green chili, coriander, and cumin. Roll mixture in cabbage leaves. Heat oil and shallow-fry rolls until golden on both sides, season with lemon juice.

name: Dry arbi (Sookhi arbi)
instructions: Boil and peel arbi, cut into pieces. Heat oil, add cumin and asafoetida, add arbi pieces with turmeric and red chili. Fry on high heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until crispy and browned.

name: Masala arbi
instructions: Boil and peel arbi, cut into pieces. Heat oil, add onions and ginger. Add arbi with tomatoes, turmeric, coriander, and cumin. Simmer for 15 minutes until arbi is fully cooked and flavors blend.

name: Peas brinjal bhartha (Matar baingan bhartha)
instructions: Roast eggplant until soft, peel and mash. Heat oil, add onions and ginger, add peas and roasted eggplant. Add tomatoes and spices, cook for 10 minutes until well combined.

name: Okra/Lady's fingers fry (Bhindi sabzi/sabji/subji)
instructions: Trim okra and cut into pieces. Heat oil, add onions and ginger. Add okra with turmeric and red chili, cook on high heat for 10 minutes, stirring constantly. Season with salt and lemon juice.

name: Beans foogath
instructions: Trim and chop beans finely. Heat oil, add cumin, asafoetida, and curry leaves. Add beans with red chili, cook for 8 minutes. Add grated coconut and cook for 2 more minutes, season with salt.

name: Cauliflower, pea and potato bhujia (Phoolgobhi, matar aur aloo bhujia)
instructions: Chop cauliflower, peas, and potatoes into small pieces. Heat oil, add cumin and asafoetida, add vegetables with turmeric. Cook on high heat for 12 minutes, stirring frequently, until all vegetables are cooked and lightly fried.

name: Vegetables stir fry
instructions: Chop vegetables into uniform pieces. Heat oil in a wok, add ginger and garlic. Stir-fry vegetables for 5 minutes, add soy sauce, vinegar, and cornstarch slurry. Toss until well coated and serve hot.

name: Broccoli delight
instructions: Chop broccoli into florets and blanch. Heat oil, add minced garlic and broccoli, toss for 3 minutes. Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice. Optional: toss with fried breadcrumbs for crunch.

name: Dhansak (vegetarian)
instructions: Cook lentils and pumpkin until tender. Sauté onion and garlic, add tomato and cooked lentils. Simmer until creamy. Serve hot.

name: Cauliflower basket
instructions: Blanch cauliflower florets. Beat eggs with milk and cheese, pour over cauliflower in baking dish. Bake at 180°C for 20 minutes until golden.

name: Stuffed bottle gourd (Stuffed ghiya/lauki)
instructions: Hollow bottle gourd pieces. Stuff with spiced paneer-onion-chilli mixture. Shallow fry until gourd is tender and filling is cooked through.

name: Stuffed baked potatoes
instructions: Bake potatoes until tender. Scoop out centers and mix with cottage cheese and herbs. Refill and bake again for 10 minutes.

name: Fricassee of Mushroom
instructions: Sauté sliced mushrooms in butter until golden. Add onion and garlic, cook until soft. Add cream and simmer until sauce thickens. Season and serve.

name: Cauliflower au gratin
instructions: Blanch cauliflower. Make cheese sauce with butter and milk. Layer cauliflower in baking dish, pour sauce, top with breadcrumbs and cheese. Bake at 180°C until golden.

name: Spinach souffle
instructions: Blanch and chop spinach. Make béchamel sauce, mix with spinach and egg yolks. Fold in stiffly beaten egg whites. Bake at 190°C until puffed and golden.

name: Beetroot and egg salad (Chukandar aur ande ka salad)
instructions: Boil and cube beetroot and eggs. Mix with finely chopped onion, lime juice and fresh coriander. Season with salt and serve chilled.

name: Chicken and tomato towers
instructions: Layer grilled chicken breast slices with tomato slices and fresh mozzarella. Drizzle with olive oil and basil. Serve at room temperature.

name: Carrot raita (Gajar ka raita)
instructions: Grate carrot finely. Mix into yogurt with roasted cumin seeds, chilli powder and salt. Garnish with coriander and serve chilled.

name: Pumpkin raita (Kaddu ka raita)
instructions: Boil and grate pumpkin, cool completely. Mix into yogurt with roasted cumin, chilli powder and salt. Serve chilled.

name: Mint and peanut raita (Pudinay aur moongfali ka raita)
instructions: Coarsely crush roasted peanuts. Mix into yogurt with finely chopped mint, chilli powder, salt and lime juice. Serve chilled.

name: Spinach raita (Palak ka raita)
instructions: Blanch, squeeze and finely chop spinach. Mix into yogurt with roasted cumin, chilli powder, salt and lemon juice. Serve chilled.

name: Bathua raita
instructions: Blanch, squeeze and mince bathua leaves. Fold into yogurt with roasted cumin, chilli powder, salt and fresh coriander. Serve chilled.

name: Chicken pakora/pakoda
instructions: Marinate boneless chicken pieces in gram flour batter with spices. Deep fry until golden brown and crispy. Serve hot with mint chutney.

name: Fish pakora/pakoda
instructions: Cut fish into pieces and coat with spiced gram flour batter. Deep fry until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels and serve hot.

name: Plain urad dal vada (Uzunne vada/Minapa garelu/Ulundu vadai/Medu vada)
instructions: Soak urad dal for 30 minutes. Grind to a fluffy batter with minimal water. Add spices and shape into rings. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown.

name: Instant dhokla
instructions: Mix gram flour, semolina and yogurt with spices and baking soda. Pour into a greased pan and steam for 10-12 minutes until cooked through. Cut into squares and serve hot.

name: Tomato sandwich (grilled)
instructions: Butter bread slices lightly. Layer sliced tomatoes and grated cheese between bread. Close sandwich and grill in a pan until bread is crispy and cheese melts.

name: French sandwich (toasted)
instructions: Whisk eggs with milk and a pinch of cinnamon. Dip bread slices in the mixture and toast on a buttered griddle until golden. Serve with a spread of choice.

name: Mint and peanut sandwich
instructions: Spread peanut butter on bread. Add finely chopped fresh mint leaves. Drizzle with honey and a touch of butter. Toast lightly if desired.

name: Chicken walnut sandwich (Chicken aur akhrot ke sandwich)
instructions: Mix shredded cooked chicken with crushed walnuts and mayonnaise. Spread on bread with chopped green chilli. Close sandwich and toast until crispy.

name: Submarine sandwich
instructions: Split submarine bread lengthwise. Layer cheese, lettuce, and tomato slices inside. Add mayonnaise and olive oil. Toast in oven or pan until cheese melts and bread crisps.

name: Cheese open sandwich
instructions: Toast bread slice lightly. Butter it and top with grated cheese, tomato slice, and lettuce. Place under grill until cheese melts. Serve immediately.

name: Sunset and sunrise open sandwich
instructions: Toast bread slice. Butter it lightly. Top with a fried egg and grated cheese. Add tomato slice on top. Place under grill briefly until cheese melts.

name: Danish luncheon sandwich
instructions: Toast bread. Spread butter and add sliced ham or luncheon meat. Layer cheese, tomato, and lettuce. Close sandwich and serve.

name: Chicken and corn open sandwich
instructions: Toast bread and butter lightly. Top with shredded cooked chicken mixed with corn kernels and mayonnaise. Sprinkle cheese and grill until melted.

name: Savoury puffs
instructions: Make choux pastry with flour, butter, salt, and water. Pipe into mounds on a baking sheet. Bake at 200°C for 20-25 minutes until puffed and golden.

name: Savoury cheese horns
instructions: Make or use ready-made puff pastry. Roll thin and coat with grated cheese and seasonings. Roll into horn shapes and bake at 200°C for 15-20 minutes.

name: Paneer patties
instructions: Crumble paneer and mashed potato. Mix with spices and finely chopped ginger-green chilli. Shape into patties and shallow fry until golden brown.

name: Vegetable patties
instructions: Grate cabbage and carrot. Mix with cooked peas, spices, and gram flour as binder. Shape into patties and shallow fry until edges are crispy.

name: Rice dal porridge (Chawal dal ki khichdi/khichri)
instructions: Roast rice and lentils together. Add water and turmeric. Cook until soft and porridge-like. Temper with ghee, ginger, and cumin seeds.

name: Lentils and semolina porridge (Dal suji porridge/daliya)
instructions: Roast semolina in ghee until fragrant. Add roasted lentils and water. Cook until soft and creamy. Temper with cumin and ginger.

name: Spinach khichri (Palak khichri/khichdi)
instructions: Cook rice and lentils with turmeric. Add chopped spinach in the last 5 minutes of cooking. Finish with ghee and ginger tempering.

name: Lentils and wheat porridge (Dal wheat porridge/daliya )
instructions: Roast wheat and lentils. Cook with water until soft. Add ghee and temper with cumin seeds and ginger. Serve warm.

name: Maize porridge
instructions: Boil maize (corn) with water until soft. Add milk and cook further. Finish with ghee, a touch of jaggery, and cardamom powder.

name: Fermented bengal gram vada (Khameerikrit/Ufna hua channa dal ka vada)
instructions: Soak and ferment bengal gram and urad dal for 4-6 hours. Grind to a fluffy batter with spices. Shape and deep fry until golden.

name: Besan bathua chilla/cheela
instructions: Mix gram flour with finely chopped bathua leaves and spices. Make a thick batter. Pour spoonfuls on a hot griddle with ghee and cook until light brown.

name: Gram flour and semolina dhokla (Besan suji dhokla)
instructions: Mix gram flour, semolina, and yogurt with spices. Add baking soda just before steaming. Pour into a greased pan and steam for 12-15 minutes.

name: Soya roti
instructions: Mix soya flour and wheat flour with salt. Add water to form a soft dough. Rest for 15 minutes. Roll into thin rotlis and cook on a hot griddle until golden brown spots appear.

name: Sprouts upma
instructions: Heat ghee and temper mustard seeds. Add onions and saute. Add boiled sprouts, peas, and diced carrots. Stir in semolina and toast lightly. Add salt and mix well until combined.

name: Poshtik chilla/cheela
instructions: Mix gram flour with yogurt, salt, and turmeric. Add finely chopped vegetables. Heat ghee on a griddle and pour the batter to form a thin pancake. Cook until golden on both sides.

name: Creamed spinach
instructions: Blanch spinach and puree finely. Heat butter and saute minced garlic. Add spinach puree and stir constantly. Add cream, nutmeg, salt and pepper. Simmer for 5 minutes until creamy.

name: Prawn sandwich
instructions: Cook prawns with lemon and spices. Toast bread slices lightly. Layer with lettuce, tomato, cucumber, and cooked prawns. Spread mayonnaise and salt to taste.

name: Fish sandwich
instructions: Pan-fry or grill fish until cooked through. Toast bread and layer with lettuce, tomato, and cooked fish. Apply mayonnaise and lemon juice to taste.

name: Kidney bean sandwich with cottage cheese
instructions: Boil and mash kidney beans with a fork. Mix with crumbled cottage cheese and salt. Toast bread and layer with lettuce, tomato, and bean-cottage cheese mixture.

name: Afghani chicken
instructions: Marinate chicken chunks in yogurt, cream, ginger-garlic paste, and kasuri methi for 30 minutes. Grill or bake at 200°C until cooked through, about 20 minutes.

name: Hariyali Fish Tikka
instructions: Make a green paste with coriander, mint, and green chili. Mix with yogurt and ginger. Marinate fish pieces for 20 minutes. Grill or bake until cooked and slightly charred.

name: Handi chicken
instructions: Sear chicken pieces and set aside. In the same pot, make a paste of ginger and garlic. Add spices, cream, and butter. Return chicken and simmer covered for 20 minutes on low heat.

name: Lemon chicken
instructions: Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces. Stir-fry with minced ginger and garlic. Add soy sauce, chili sauce, and lemon juice. Toss until chicken is coated and cooked through, about 10 minutes.

name: Baked eggs in tomato sauce
instructions: Make a tomato sauce by cooking onions, garlic, and crushed tomatoes. Pour into baking dish. Create wells and crack eggs into them. Drizzle cream and bake at 180°C for 12-15 minutes.

name: Spinach mushroom scrambled egg (Palak mushroom bhurji)
instructions: Chop spinach and mushrooms finely. Heat oil and saute onion and garlic. Add vegetables and cook until soft. Pour beaten eggs and scramble until fully cooked. Season with salt and pepper.

name: Corn omelette/omlet
instructions: Beat eggs with salt and pepper. Add corn kernels and diced vegetables. Heat butter on a pan and pour mixture. Let it set slightly, then fold and cook until done. Top with cheese.

name: Cajun chicken
instructions: Coat chicken with a blend of paprika, cayenne, garlic powder, and thyme. Pan-sear or grill until cooked through. The coating should be slightly charred and flavorful.

name: Paaner do pyaza
instructions: Cut paneer into cubes. Stir-fry with julienned onions, capsicum, and tomato. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook until vegetables are tender. Season with salt and spices.

name: Paneer lababdar
instructions: Make a tomato base by cooking crushed tomatoes with ginger-garlic paste. Add cream and paneer cubes. Simmer for 10 minutes. Sprinkle kasuri methi before serving.

name: Cabbage raita (Pattagobhi raita)
instructions: Finely shred or grate cabbage. Mix with yogurt and salt. Temper cumin seeds in hot oil and pour over. Add chopped coriander and green chili. Mix well.

name: Pomegranate raita (Anaar ka raita)
instructions: Whisk yogurt with salt until smooth. Add pomegranate arils and gently mix. Dust with roasted cumin powder and chaat masala. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Chicken and cheese souffle
instructions: Mix cooked shredded chicken with cream. Beat egg whites until stiff peaks form. Fold in beaten yolks with cheese and chicken mixture. Bake in a buttered dish at 190°C for 20 minutes.

name: Khakhra
instructions: Make a dough with wheat flour, oil, and salt. Roll very thin into circles. Cook on a griddle until crisp, flipping occasionally. Season with spices and store in an airtight container.

name: Quinoa khichdi/khichri
instructions: Rinse quinoa and cook with water in a 2:1 ratio. In a separate pan, temper cumin seeds and add chopped vegetables. Combine cooked quinoa with vegetables and mix well.

name: Semolina dhokla (Suji/Rava dhokla)
instructions: Mix semolina with yogurt and salt. Add minced ginger and green chili. Let it sit for 15 minutes. Pour into a greased pan and steam for 15 minutes. Cut into squares and serve.

name: Ham and Bean soup
instructions: Sauté diced onion and garlic. Add chopped ham and beans. Pour vegetable or chicken broth and simmer for 20 minutes. Season with salt and herbs to taste.

name: Whey soup
instructions: Heat whey with water. Add finely chopped vegetables like carrots and peas. Simmer until vegetables are tender. Season with salt, pepper, and dried herbs.

name: Paneer soup
instructions: Cube paneer and vegetables. Heat broth with minced garlic. Add vegetables and paneer. Simmer for 10 minutes. Add cream and seasonings. Serve hot.

name: Garlic soup
instructions: Roast whole garlic cloves and peel them. Mash and add to heated broth. Simmer for 10 minutes. Add cream and butter. Season with salt, pepper, and fresh herbs.

name: Dal makhani
instructions: Soak and cook lentils and beans until soft. Make a tomato base with ginger-garlic paste. Add cooked lentils and beans. Stir in cream and butter. Simmer for 15 minutes.

name: Dalma
instructions: Cook lentils with turmeric until tender. In another pot, cook mixed vegetables with cumin tempering. Combine lentils and vegetables. Simmer together for 10 minutes.

name: Split bengal gram dal (Channa dal)
instructions: Rinse split bengal gram and boil with turmeric until tender. Temper mustard and cumin seeds in hot oil. Add ginger-garlic paste and saute. Mix with cooked lentils.

name: Horsegram dal (Kulthi dal)
instructions: Soak horsegram for 30 minutes. Pressure cook with water, turmeric, and salt until tender. Temper with cumin and chili seeds in hot oil and add to cooked dal. Stir and serve.

name: Panchmel dal
instructions: Mix all five dals and soak for 20 minutes. Pressure cook with water, turmeric, and salt until soft. Temper with cumin, chili, and asafetida in hot oil. Mix into dal and serve.

name: Dal dhokli
instructions: Make a stiff dough from wheat flour, salt, and oil. Cook moong dal with turmeric and ginger. Roll thin dough sheets, cut into strips, and drop into simmering dal. Cook until strips are soft and dal thickens. Serve hot.

name: Maa chaane ki dal
instructions: Soak black chickpeas overnight. Pressure cook with water, turmeric, and salt until very tender. Temper with cumin seeds, dried chili, and ginger in hot oil. Add to cooked chickpeas and mix well. Serve hot.

name: Garlic chickpea soup (Lahasun aur chane ka shoraba)
instructions: Boil cooked chickpeas with ginger-garlic paste, cumin, turmeric, and diced tomato in water. Simmer for 15 minutes to blend flavors. Season with salt and drizzle oil. Serve as a light soup.

name: Malvani Egg Curry
instructions: Heat kadhai and cook sliced onions on high flame until they start picking up colour. Add garlic, ginger, grated coconut and pinch of salt, mix well and cook until coconut turns golden brown. Make a small well in the centre, add oil and toast black peppercorns, coriander seeds, cinnamon, and cloves directly into it. Mix and roast until all spices are toasted but not burnt. Transfer to bowl and let cool completely. Blend cooled mixture with water into a fine smooth paste. Heat kadhai with oil on high flame. Add kashmiri red chilli powder and Malvani masala and stir quickly. Add the prepared coconut masala paste and cook on high flame stirring continuously until oil separates. Add hot water to bring to gravy consistency. Bring to a full boil, then simmer for 6-7 minutes until oil rises and consistency is rich and smooth. Taste and adjust salt. Poke boiled eggs all over with a knife so gravy can seep in. Add eggs to gravy and mix gently. Add fresh coriander and stir through. Serve hot with rice, bhakri, or vade.

name: Sindhi Sanna Pakoda
ingredients: oil as required for deep frying
instructions: For the batter, take a large bowl and add besan, diced onion, green chillies, and fresh coriander. Add coriander seeds, cumin seeds, turmeric powder, spicy red chilli powder, baking soda, and salt. Using clean hands, press and mix everything together well so the onion releases a little moisture and everything combines. Add water gradually and mix until you get a dropping consistency batter that falls off the spoon in thick drops. Cover the batter and let rest. For the chutney, add fresh coriander, mint, roughly cut onion, green chillies, ginger, garlic, tamarind pulp, jaggery, black salt, cumin powder, spicy red chilli powder, salt, ice cubes, and water to a mixer jar. Grind into a fine smooth paste first. Transfer to a bowl and add water gradually to thin it out to a pourable, drinkable consistency. Taste and adjust salt. Refrigerate until ready to serve. Heat oil to 160-165 degrees Celsius for the first fry. Add a tablespoon of hot oil from the frying pan into the batter and mix it in. Carefully drop portions of batter into the oil using your hands. As soon as they go in you will see them rise to the surface and puff up. Fry on medium flame for 2-3 minutes until just a light colour develops on the outside. Remove with a strainer and let them rest for 3-4 minutes. Once rested, break each pakoda in half, then break the larger piece again. You will see the inside is slightly underdone. Break all the pakodas the same way. Now heat the oil to 175-180 degrees Celsius for the second fry. Add all the broken pakodas and fry until they are deeply golden brown and completely crispy all the way through. Remove with a strainer and drain well. Serve hot with the chutney and pav or bread slices alongside.

name: Hyderabadi Chicken Dum Biryani
instructions: First marination: Mix chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, and salt. Let marinate for 1-2 hours. Soak basmati rice in water for 30 minutes. Prepare biryani masala by dry roasting bay leaves, cinnamon, cloves, black cardamom, green cardamom, and black peppercorns until fragrant. Prepare birista by thinly slicing onions and deep frying in ghee until golden brown and crispy. Remove and drain on paper towels. For the second marination, mix the marinated chicken with fresh coriander, mint, green chilli, and some of the prepared birista. Let sit for 30 minutes. Boil water with the biryani masala, salt, and a few birista pieces. Add the soaked rice and cook until 70 percent done (parboil). In a heavy-bottomed pot or biryani handi, layer the marinated chicken first, spreading it evenly. Layer the parboiled rice on top. Top with remaining birista pieces and fresh coriander. Soak saffron in warm milk and pour over the rice layer. Cover the pot with foil, then place the heavy lid on top to create a seal. Place the covered pot on high heat for 2-3 minutes until steam forms, then reduce heat to low and cook for 40-45 minutes. Turn off the heat and let rest for 5-7 minutes without opening the lid. Carefully open the lid and gently fluff the biryani with a fork. Serve hot with raita and shorba if desired.

name: dahi aloo
instructions: Shallow fry baby potatoes until golden brown. In the same oil, temper cumin seeds and black cardamom. Add chopped onions and cook until soft. Add turmeric, red chilli powder, and black pepper. Add curd mixed with coriander powder and cumin powder. Add fried potatoes, garam masala, and bay leaves. Cook covered on low heat for 15-20 minutes until potatoes are tender and flavors meld. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: onion capsicum sabzi with tikona paratha
instructions: For sabzi: Heat oil, temper cumin seeds. Add sliced onions and capsicum, cook until softened. Add turmeric, cumin powder, coriander powder, red chilli powder, and black pepper. Stir in curd mixed with garam masala. Cook on low heat for 8-10 minutes until vegetables are tender and gravy coats them. For paratha: Knead wheat flour with water and salt. Roll into triangles, brush with ghee, fold into smaller triangles. Cook on griddle with ghee until golden brown on both sides. Serve sabzi hot with parathas.

name: oreo mango custard
instructions: Heat 1 litre milk in a heavy-bottomed pan. Mix custard powder with 4 tbsp milk separately to form a smooth paste. When milk is warm, add the custard paste and stir continuously to avoid lumps. Cook on medium heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring constantly. Add sugar and mango pulp, mix well. Remove from heat and let cool completely. In serving glasses, layer crushed oreo biscuits at the bottom. Pour custard mixture and top with mango chunks. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving. Garnish with oreo crumbs and fresh mango pieces.

name: corn cheese balls
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Mix with boiled corn, chopped onion, capsicum, green chilli, oregano, chilli flakes, and black pepper. Add mozzarella cheese and mix well. Form small balls from the mixture. In a separate bowl, mix corn flour and maida. Heat oil for deep frying. Roll each potato-corn ball in the flour mixture and deep fry until golden brown on all sides. Remove with slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Serve hot with tomato ketchup or green chutney.

name: bihari thali
instructions: Cook rice in water with turmeric and salt. For dal: Cook arhar dal with water, onion, and tomato. Temper with ghee, garlic, and cumin seeds. For aloo bhujiya: Shallow fry sliced potatoes with mustard oil, cumin seeds, and dry red chilli. For eggplant curry: Fry eggplant slices and chanadal wadi, add mustard seeds, garlic, green chilli, turmeric, and tomato puree. For bajka: Mix besan with rice flour, spices, add lauki and brinjal slices, and shallow fry until crispy. Serve all components together as a complete Bihari meal.

name: biscoff cheesecake
instructions: Crush biscoff biscuits finely and mix with 2 tbsp melted butter. Press mixture into the base of serving glasses or a springform pan to form a crust. In a bowl, beat cream cheese until smooth and creamy. Add caster sugar and vanilla essence, mix well. Whip the whipping cream to stiff peaks and gently fold into the cream cheese mixture. Layer half the cream mixture over biscuit base. Swirl in biscoff spread, then add remaining cream mixture on top. Refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight until set. Drizzle with biscoff spread and garnish with crushed biscuits before serving.

name: moong dosa
instructions: For dosa: Soak moong dal for 30 minutes. Grind moong dal with coriander leaves, green chilli, garlic, ginger, cumin seeds, and turmeric into a smooth batter with water. Add salt to taste. Heat griddle and lightly oil it. Pour ladle of batter and spread thin. Cook on medium heat until edges turn crispy and bottom is golden. Flip and cook other side briefly. For chutney: Heat oil, temper mustard seeds, add chana dal and urad dal. Add minced ginger, garlic, and dry red chilli. Add chopped tomato and cook until soft. Add salt and sugar. Grind to coarse chutney consistency. Serve hot dosa with chutney and onion garnish.

name: Healthy Fish Paturi
instructions: Clean fish pieces. Blend coriander, mint, curd, garlic, green chilli, and ginger into a smooth paste. Marinate fish pieces with this paste. Wrap in banana leaf and steam until fish is cooked through. Serve hot with lemon wedges.

name: Whole Wheat Chocolate Cupcake
instructions: Combine dry ingredients: whole wheat flour, milk powder, cocoa powder, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Mix wet ingredients: milk, ghee, sugar, and vanilla essence. Gently combine wet and dry ingredients until just mixed. Pour into cupcake molds. Bake at 350F for 20 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.

name: Goan Prawn Curry
instructions: Soak tamarind. Toast coriander seeds lightly. Grind fresh coconut, garlic, ginger, red chilli, and toasted coriander seeds into a fine paste. Heat coconut oil in a pan. Add the masala paste and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add prawns and cook until they turn pink. Add tamarind juice and kokam. Simmer for 5 minutes. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve with steam rice.

name: Cheesy Paneer Cutlet
instructions: Crumble paneer finely. Finely dice all vegetables (onion, carrot, beans, cauliflower, peas). Mix paneer, diced vegetables, cheese, mixed herbs, chilli flakes, and salt. Shape mixture into oval cutlet shapes. Mix corn flour and all purpose flour together. Coat each cutlet with flour mixture, then dip in bread crumbs. Shallow fry or deep fry on both sides until golden brown.

name: Maddur Vada
instructions: Mix rice flour, sooji, maida, roasted peanuts, red chilli powder, coriander powder, asafoetida, and salt. Finely mince ginger, onion, green chilli, coriander leaves, and curry leaves. Add minced ingredients to dry mixture. Add oil and water gradually to form a dough that holds together. Shape into flat round vadas. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown and crispy on all sides.

name: Mango Pickle (Aam Ka Achar)
instructions: Cut raw mangoes into pieces. Dry roast mustard seeds, fennel seeds, cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, nigella seeds, and carom seeds separately until fragrant. Mix all roasted seeds with turmeric powder, kashmiri chilli powder, and asafoetida. Layer mango pieces with salt and spice mixture in a clean glass jar. Pour mustard oil and vinegar over the layers. Close the jar tightly and keep in the sun for fermentation. Stir occasionally with a clean spoon. The pickle will be ready in 3-4 weeks and can be stored for a year.

name: Lauki Handva
instructions: Mix sooji, grated lauki, and curd in a bowl. Add water gradually to achieve a pancake batter consistency. Finely dice onion and capsicum. Chop green chilli finely. Add diced vegetables, chopped curry leaves, and salt to the batter. Heat oil in a pan and temper mustard seeds. Add tempered seeds to batter. Just before cooking, add baking soda and mix gently. Pour spoonfuls onto a warm griddle and shallow fry on both sides until golden brown.

name: Rasmalai
instructions: Boil 1.5 litres of milk. Add white vinegar while stirring continuously to curdle the milk. Strain through cheesecloth to obtain paneer/chena. Knead the chena until smooth. Roll into small balls (rasgulla). Dissolve 1.5 cups sugar in 2-3 cups water to make syrup. Boil syrup and carefully drop rasgulla balls into it. Simmer for 20-30 minutes until they puff up and become soft. For the rabri: Boil 1/5 litre milk and reduce by half. Add 1/2 cup sugar, cardamom powder, and saffron. Cool the rabri. Remove rasgulla from sugar syrup and soak in chilled rabri. Top with chopped dry fruits and serve chilled.

name: Makhana and Oats Laddoo
instructions: Toast makhana and oats separately until golden. Roast almonds and cashews. Heat ghee and melt jaggery in it. Combine all toasted and roasted ingredients with the jaggery-ghee mixture along with melon seeds, pumpkin seeds, ginger powder, and cinnamon powder. Mix well and form into laddoos while still warm. Cool before serving.

name: Bengali Moong Dal Khichdi
instructions: Rinse rice and moong dal. Heat mustard oil in a pan, add bay leaves, cloves, green cardamom, cinnamon stick, and dry red chillies. Add ginger and green chilli, then diced potato and cauliflower. Sauté for 2-3 minutes. Add rice and moong dal, mix well. Add water (about 4 cups for 5 servings) and bring to boil. Simmer until rice and dal are completely cooked and khichdi is creamy. Finish with ghee and serve.

name: Bihari Drumstick Curry
instructions: Heat oil in a pan. Add yellow and brown mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add chopped garlic and dry red chillies. Add diced potato and tomato. Mix well and add turmeric and chilli powder. Add cleaned drumstick pieces (cut into 2-3 inch pieces). Sauté on high heat for 2-3 minutes, then reduce heat and cook covered until drumsticks are tender (about 20-25 minutes). Stir occasionally. Season with salt and serve hot.

name: Tangy Mango Pickle
instructions: Wash green mangoes and cut into small pieces, removing the seed. Mix mango pieces with salt and turmeric powder. Heat oil and add mustard seeds and fenugreek seeds, let them crackle. Pour this tempering over the mango pieces. Add red chilli powder and mix well. Transfer to a clean glass jar and let it ferment for 3-4 days in sunlight. Stir occasionally. Store in an airtight container.

name: Street Style Masala Pav
instructions: Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add ginger-garlic paste and chopped green chillies, sauté for 1 minute. Add diced onions and cook until translucent. Add diced tomatoes and capsicum, cook for 2-3 minutes. Add turmeric powder, pav bhaji masala, kashmiri red chilli powder, and schezwan chutney. Mix well and cook for 3-4 minutes. Toast the pav with butter on a griddle. Serve masala on top of toasted pav, garnish with aloo bhujiya, crushed peanut, coriander leaves, and cheese slice.

name: Kulthi Ki Dal
ingredients: kulthi dal (horse gram) 1 cup soaked overnight
instructions: Pressure cook soaked kulthi dal with ginger and water (about 3-4 whistles) until cooked through. Heat oil in a separate pan, add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add bay leaves, green cardamom, black cardamom, and cinnamon stick. Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for 1 minute. Add diced onions and cook until golden. Add tomato paste and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add turmeric, cumin powder, coriander powder, red chilli powder, garam masala, and black pepper powder. Mix well. Add the cooked dal and simmer for 5-7 minutes. Finish with ghee and garnish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot.

name: Veg Dum Biryani
ingredients: water 2 cups for gravy + 3 liters for rice
saffron pinch dissolved in 0.25 cup milk
instructions: 1. Soak basmati rice for 30 minutes. 2. Prepare jhol: Heat 1 cup oil and fry 4 cups sliced onions until crispy and golden brown, reserve fried onions. 3. Toast whole spices (black cardamom, cinnamon, javitri, cardamom, cloves, black pepper, fennel, shahi jeera) for 1-2 minutes to make biryani masala. Grind to powder. 4. Marinate vegetables: Mix 1.5 cups curd with ginger-garlic paste, green chilli, turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder, salt. Add diced vegetables and marinate for 15 minutes. 5. Parboil rice: Boil 3 liters water with salt and green chillies. Add soaked rice and whole spices. Cook till 70% done (rice should have slight bite). Drain. 6. Fry vegetables: Heat 0.75 cup oil in kadhai, add marinated vegetables and cook for 8-10 minutes till they have colour. 7. Layer the biryani: In heavy-bottomed pot, spread layer of jhol (fried onions in oil). Add vegetable layer, top with parboiled rice. Repeat layers ending with rice on top. 8. Top garnish: Pour saffron milk on rice, sprinkle fried onions, add ghee (2 tbsp), rose water, kewra water. 9. Dum cooking: Cover pot with aluminium foil, place heavy lid on top. Heat on high for 2-3 minutes till steam forms. Reduce to low heat and cook for 45-60 minutes until rice is fully cooked and fluffy. 10. Open carefully and serve hot with raita.

name: Paneer Shimla Mirch
instructions: 1. Cut paneer into 3/4-inch cubes. Mix with salt, turmeric, and 1 tsp chilli powder. Let sit 10 minutes. 2. Make masala: Dry roast cumin seeds, coriander seeds, and peppercorns for 1-2 minutes. Grind to powder and set aside. 3. In kadhai, heat 5 tbsp mustard oil on high heat. Add 3 dry red chillis, let them flavour the oil for 30 seconds. 4. Add chopped garlic and ginger, sauté for 30 seconds. 5. Add 1 cup chopped onions, cook till golden brown (3-4 minutes). 6. Lower heat, add ground masala powder, turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, coriander powder, kitchen king masala, and salt. Cook for 1-2 minutes. 7. Add 5-6 chopped tomatoes, cook till they break down completely (5-7 minutes). 8. Add 0.5 cup water and simmer for 3-4 minutes. Set gravy aside. 9. In separate pan, heat 3 tbsp mustard oil on high heat. Carefully add marinated paneer cubes and shallow fry till light golden on all sides (2-3 minutes). 10. Cut 1 medium capsicum and 1 large onion into 1-inch bulb pieces. 11. Add fried paneer to the gravy. Add capsicum and onion pieces. 12. Cook on high heat for 3-4 minutes so vegetables stay crunchy. 13. Add kasoori methi and fresh coriander. Check salt and spices. 14. Serve hot with roti or paratha.

name: Thatte Idli
instructions: 1. Soak 2 cups rice in water for 4-6 hours. Separately soak 1 cup urad dal for 4-6 hours. 2. Drain rice and grind with just enough water to make a smooth, slightly thick batter (do not make it runny). This should take 5-7 minutes using a blender. Pour into a large bowl. 3. Drain urad dal and grind with minimal water into smooth, fluffy batter. The urad batter should be fluffier than rice batter. 4. Mix both batters together gently, folding from bottom to top to incorporate air. Add 1 tsp salt and mix gently. 5. Cover the batter bowl with a cloth and let it ferment at room temperature for 8-12 hours until it rises and becomes bubbly. 6. After fermentation, the batter will be light and airy. If using an electric steamer (Ninja Combi or similar), fill idli moulds 3/4 full with batter. 7. Steam for 10-12 minutes until a skewer inserted comes out clean and idli is cooked through. 8. Let cool for 2-3 minutes before removing from moulds. 9. Gently remove idlis onto a serving plate. 10. Serve hot with sambar and coconut chutney.

name: Corn & Cheese Sandwich
instructions: 1. Prepare the filling: Chop tomato, capsicum, and onion finely. 2. Mix chopped vegetables with 1.5 cups boiled corn nibblets. 3. Add chilli flakes, pepper powder, oregano, salt, and chopped garlic to the mixture. 4. Add 2 tbsp tomato ketchup and handful of chopped coriander. Add a dash of water if needed to bring together. Mix well and set aside. 5. Take bread slices in pairs. Spread mint chutney on both inner sides of the bread. 6. Spread generous amount of corn-cheese filling on one slice. 7. Top with generous amount of grated mozzarella cheese. 8. Cover with the second bread slice and press gently. 9. Apply 1 tbsp softened butter on outer surface of the sandwich. 10. Heat a tawa (griddle) or flat pan on medium heat. 11. Once hot, place the sandwich on tawa. Cook for 2-3 minutes till bottom is golden and crispy. 12. Flip carefully and cook other side for 2-3 minutes till golden and cheese is melted. 13. Remove from tawa and cut diagonally into two triangles. 14. Serve immediately while hot with extra ketchup, mayo, or mint chutney on the side.

name: Chana Dal Halwa
instructions: 1. Soak 1 cup chana dal in water for 30 minutes. Drain thoroughly. 2. Grind the soaked chana dal to a coarse, grainy texture (not into flour - keep it chunky). 3. In a heavy-bottomed pan, heat 0.5 cup ghee on medium heat. 4. Add the coarsely ground chana dal to the ghee. Roast continuously for 8-10 minutes, stirring often, till it turns golden brown and smells very aromatic. This roasting is crucial for flavour. 5. Meanwhile, prepare the syrup: In another pan, boil 1 cup water with 6 tbsp sugar and 10 green cardamom pods till sugar dissolves completely. Crush 1 black cardamom and add to syrup. Keep this syrup warm. 6. When dal is golden and roasted, slowly and carefully pour the warm sugar syrup into the roasted dal while stirring continuously with a wooden spoon to prevent lumps. 7. Keep stirring constantly. Add milk (1.5 cups) gradually in batches while stirring, to avoid curdling. 8. Continue cooking on medium heat, stirring constantly, till the halwa becomes thick, glossy, and leaves the sides of the pan (5-7 minutes). The texture should be of thick porridge. 9. In a small pan, heat remaining 2 tbsp ghee. 10. Fry the dry fruits (chopped cashews, almonds, and pista) in ghee till golden. Remove and set aside. 11. Pour the hot halwa into a serving bowl or greased plate. 12. Top with roasted dry fruits and saffron-soaked milk (optional). 13. Serve warm while it is still soft. Can also be spread on a plate to set for 30 minutes, then cut into squares.

name: Dahi Wali Bhindi
instructions: 1. Wash okra thoroughly and pat dry completely with a cloth - moisture makes it slimy. Cut into 1-inch pieces. 2. In a kadhai or wok, heat 3 tbsp mustard oil on high heat till smoking. 3. Add 0.5 tsp asafoetida and let it crackle. 4. Add 3-4 chopped green chillis and 2 tsp chopped ginger. Sauté briefly. 5. Add all the dried okra pieces. Cook on high heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring often, till okra is slightly crispy and loses its stickiness. 6. Add salt and 1 tsp black salt. Add 1.5 tsp amchur or chaat masala. 7. Cook for 2-3 more minutes, stirring well. Remove and set aside to cool. 8. Prepare the yogurt masala: In a bowl, whisk 1 cup curd with a fork till smooth. 9. To the curd, add salt, 0.5 tsp black salt, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp Kashmiri mirch powder, 2 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, and 1 tbsp gram flour. Mix well to combine. 10. In another pan, heat 2 tbsp mustard oil on medium heat. 11. Add 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, let them splutter. 12. Add 1 tbsp chopped ginger and 1 tbsp chopped garlic. Sauté for 1 minute. 13. Add 2 chopped green chillis. 14. Add 0.5 cup chopped onions and cook till golden brown (3-4 minutes). 15. Slowly pour the yogurt-spice mixture into the cooked onions while stirring constantly to prevent curdling. 16. Keep stirring for 2-3 minutes till mixture is well combined and heating evenly. 17. Add 1.5 cups water gradually while stirring. 18. Add the pre-cooked okra pieces to the yogurt curry. Stir gently to combine. 19. Cook on medium heat for 10-15 minutes till gravy thickens slightly. 20. Add 0.5 tsp kasoori methi powder. 21. Taste and adjust salt and spices as needed. 22. Finish with handful of fresh coriander leaves. 23. Serve hot with roti or steamed rice.

name: Baingan Kachri
instructions: Mix whole wheat flour, semolina, black salt, chilli powder, turmeric, kasoori methi, and chaat masala. Slice brinjal into rounds. Coat brinjal slices with the spice mixture, adding water to make a coating paste. Shallow fry brinjal slices in mustard oil until crispy and golden on both sides. Serve hot with chutney or curd.

name: Dhaniya Paratha
instructions: Combine wheat flour, gram flour, cumin, salt, turmeric, chilli powder, and chaat masala in a bowl. Add chopped ginger, green chillies, onion, fresh coriander, and mint leaves. Mix evenly. Add ghee and gradually knead with water into soft dough. Rest for 15-20 minutes. Divide into 4 portions and shape into balls. Roll each into a paratha using a rolling pin. Heat tawa on medium flame and cook paratha, applying ghee or oil on both sides until golden brown spots appear. Serve hot with curd, pickle, or butter.

name: Mango Kulfi
